matches.match_rationale: The core technical problem is that 10-15 campus databases each have their own schema, naming conventions, and ID formats. AI-assisted schema mapping and field normalization can automatically infer correspondences between heterogeneous schemas (e.g. 'StudentID' vs 'AlumID' vs 'PersonRef') and standardize them into a unified canonical schema. This directly addresses the inconsistency layer before any deduplication can occur.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-powered ETL layer that ingests campus exports, auto-maps fields to a master alumni schema, and flags ambiguous mappings for human review — enabling a unified alumni master list without requiring campuses to change their local systems.

matches.match_rationale: Probabilistic record linkage — determining whether two structured records refer to the same person — is a classification problem over feature vectors (name similarity, DOB match, campus history overlap, contact info). For alumni who attended multiple campuses or transferred, this is precisely the deduplication challenge: classifying record pairs as 'same person' or 'different person' across incompatible ID systems.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A record-linkage classifier trained on known duplicate pairs that scores all cross-campus record pairs, surfacing high-confidence merges automatically and flagging borderline cases for a data steward to confirm — dramatically reducing the manual effort of building a unified master list.

matches.match_rationale: Inconsistent naming conventions (nicknames, maiden names, abbreviated given names, transliterated names) are a primary barrier to automated deduplication. NER and entity normalization can canonicalize person names, extract and standardize identifiers (student IDs, phone numbers, emails) across records, and resolve ambiguities that prevent simple string matching from working.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A name and identifier normalization pipeline that standardizes all alumni name variants and contact details to canonical forms before the deduplication step, increasing the precision and recall of downstream record matching.

matches.match_rationale: Reconciling alumni records across 10-15 systems is a multi-step workflow: extract from each source, normalize, deduplicate, merge, write to master, and flag exceptions — with branching logic for edge cases (partial matches, conflicting data). A task automation agent can orchestrate this entire pipeline end-to-end on a scheduled or triggered basis, replacing what is currently a manual or ad-hoc process.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An agentic data reconciliation pipeline that runs on a schedule, pulls incremental updates from each campus system, applies the normalization and dedup logic, updates the master alumni record, and generates an exception report for human review.
